prompt: This Factory method always creates a Circle even when 'square' is requested. What is the bug?
choices: Circle and Square must be declared as enums for a Factory to work
String comparison with == checks reference identity, not content — use type.equals("circle") and type.equals("square")
The factory method must be an instance method, not static
ShapeFactory must implement an interface to qualify as a Factory pattern
explanation: type == "circle" compares references. String literals may or may not be the same reference depending on how the String was created, so this comparison is unreliable. In this case 'square' doesn't match either == check, and the method throws IllegalArgumentException rather than returning a Square. Fix: use type.equals("circle") and type.equals("square").
